description_long: This is a large, open-sided pavilion-style tent with a peaked, four-sided roof made of tan fabric, supported by four sturdy vertical poles at each corner. The tent is set on a rectangular patch of green ground, resembling grass or artificial turf, which serves as its base. The roof fabric appears taut and weather-resistant, with a subtle texture and slight overhang at the edges for rain runoff. The supporting poles are likely made of wood or metal, finished in a brown color to complement the roof. There are no visible sidewalls, doors, or windows, making the interior fully accessible from all sides. The structure is designed for shade and shelter in outdoor urban environments such as parks, plazas, or event spaces.
description: A large, open-sided pavilion tent with a peaked tan fabric roof and four brown support poles, set on a green base. The tent provides shade and shelter, with no sidewalls or visible entrances.
description_view_0: Front view showing the peaked tan roof, two front support poles, and the open, accessible interior.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, with the same peaked roof and open structure, highlighting the symmetry.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same open access and visible support poles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the open pavilion design and peaked roof, with no distinguishing features from other sides.
attribute_entrance_type: open-sided, accessible from all directions with no doors or flaps; entry is unobstructed on all four sides.
attribute_guy_line_configuration: No guy lines are visible; the tent is supported solely by four corner poles.
